title: The Docket - Week 6
subject: 3 firms got hacked through their AI tools. Here's what went wrong.
preheader: Plus: the free compliance checklist every firm should steal
bodyHtml:
The Docket

LegalTech AI Hub · Week of May 13, 2026

AI security isn’t theoretical anymore. Three firms found out the hard way this month. Here’s the breakdown.

🔓 Prompt Injection Attacks Hit Law Firms

Three mid-size firms reported data exposure incidents linked to prompt injection attacks on their AI tools. The attack vector: opposing counsel embedded hidden instructions in documents sent during discovery. When the firms’ AI tools processed these documents, the injected prompts caused the tools to include confidential client information in their outputs. This is the legal industry’s first documented prompt injection breach. If your AI tool processes documents from external parties, you need to be thinking about this.

📊 New Report: AI Saves Firms 33% on Discovery Costs

A new RAND Corporation study analyzed 120 matters across 40 firms and found that AI-assisted document review reduced discovery costs by an average of 33% while maintaining equivalent accuracy. The biggest gains were in large-scale reviews (100K+ documents), where AI cut review time by 45%. The data is in: AI discovery review isn’t just faster, it’s cheaper. Compare this to our case study data.

🇺🇸 New York Bar Launches AI Competence Requirement

Starting January 2027, New York attorneys will be required to complete at least 1 CLE credit in “technology competence,” with AI-specific options available. It’s a modest requirement, but it’s a signal: AI literacy is moving from optional to mandatory. Other state bars are expected to follow. Brush up with our AI glossary for lawyers.

Tool Spotlight

Doc Diff Tool — Now With Redline Export

We shipped an update this week: Doc Diff now exports highlighted comparisons as a clean redline document you can share with clients or attach to emails. No more screenshots of comparison views. Upload two versions, get a professional redline in seconds.

Try Doc Diff →

Stay secure out there. And if you’re processing external documents through AI tools, please read up on prompt injection. This one’s serious.

You’re receiving this because you subscribed to LegalTech AI Hub updates.
